title: bridge.py: Fire-and-forget asyncio tasks lose exceptions silently
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/kiss/bridge.py
Lines: 160, 172, 182, 195, 201

Multiple asyncio.create_task() calls without storing references:
- _handle_aprs_message_tx line 160
- _handle_aprs_ack_tx line 172
- _handle_aprs_rej_tx line 182
- _handle_raw_ax25_tx line 195
- _handle_raw_tx line 201

If any of these tasks raise exceptions, they are silently lost. The exception goes unobserved and is only logged as 'Task exception was never retrieved' when the task is garbage collected.

Impact: Link layer errors, network failures, and send failures are silently ignored, making debugging difficult and potentially leaving message delivery in an inconsistent state.

Fix: Store task references in a set and add a done_callback to handle exceptions properly, or use asyncio.TaskGroup in Python 3.11+.
